answer:
The royalists are afraid of showing too much commitment because they fear backing the "wrong horse" in the coming revolution, which could cause them to lose their standing and fortune. This hesitation allows the Rougons, who have insider information about Napoleon III's imminent coup, to appear boldly loyal and win the admiration of these cautious elites. Thus, the royalists' fear of political miscalculation creates an opportunity for the Rougons to advance their own ambitions.

\boxed{\text{They fear backing the "wrong horse" and losing their standing and fortune.}}
